title: Bachelor of Arts (Journalism and Media)
content: The BA in Journalism and Media is a cutting-edge undergraduate programme that prepares students for careers in journalism, digital media, and content creation. It combines storytelling with digital innovation, offering hands-on training in mobile journalism, podcasting, multimedia production, and social media. With newsroom simulations and real-world projects, students build strong reporting, ethical, and communication skills across print, broadcast, and digital platforms. Guided by expert faculty and industry mentors, the programme fosters critical thinking, creativity, and a global media perspective—equipping graduates to thrive as journalists, filmmakers, influencers, or strategic communicators in today’s fast-evolving media landscape. Minimum 50% marks in Class 12th. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list NOTE: For selected 3 Year Undergraduate programs, you have the option to continue for 4th year to obtain an Honours with Research Degree 01 To cultivate an understanding of media’s role in Indian society and democracy with a critical lens on authority, representation, and media ownership. 02 To develop core competencies in journalistic practice in reportage, writing, editing, fact-checking, and media ethics across platforms. 03 To train students in researching, designing, and producing a range of media products - digital storytelling, multimedia content creation, and the use of emerging tools for data journalism, mobile journalism (MoJo), digital photography and AI in newsrooms. 04 To build a strong foundation in communication theory and research methods, strategic communication and communication for development. 05 To prepare students for diverse career pathways in newsrooms, digital news and content platforms, photography and filmmaking, advertising, and media research. 01 Demonstrate professional journalistic skills in research, reporting, interviewing, and multimedia production for print, broadcast, and digital media. 02 Analyze media content with an understanding of socio-political, cultural, and economic contexts in India and the world. 03 Practice ethically responsible journalism that is fact-based, inclusive, and committed to public interest. 04 Utilize digital tools and platforms for storytelling, audience engagement, and interactivity. 05 Navigate the challenges of the contemporary Indian media industry, including disinformation, media consolidation, censorship, and technological impact. 01 Global and Industry Readiness: To develop media professionals who are adaptable to global trends, industry expectations, and entrepreneurial opportunities in journalism, filmmaking, digital strategy, and branded content. 02 Technological Adaptability: To enable students to effectively leverage emerging media technologies—including mobile journalism, podcasting, multimedia tools, and social media—to produce impactful, platform-specific content. 03 Creative and Critical Thinking: To foster innovation, curiosity, and analytical thinking that allow graduates to engage with complex social realities and produce content that informs, inspires, and influences. 01 Media Entrepreneurship: to develop entrepreneurial skills as a contemporary practitioner in journalism and media industries. 02 Transmediality: to be able to produce programs keeping Platformization in mind. Minimum 50% marks in Class 12th. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list NOTE: For selected 3 Year Undergraduate programs, you have the option to continue for 4th year to obtain an Honours with Research Degree
